title: Perfect Custardy Scrambled Eggs and Crisps
summary: Eggs are hard to master. Cooking the perfect custardy scramble, even harder, but I’m here for you. These are, no doubt, the best scrambled eggs I’ll ever have, and you can now make them at home.

method.description: Crack the eggs into a bowl, season with salt and whisk together. Set aside. Finely slice the chives and toast the bread.

Pour the eggs into a cold frying pan and add the cold butter. Put the pan over a low-medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the butter has melted and the eggs are starting to set. There should be no runny egg left – it should still be loose.

Remove from the heat and stir through the cream cheese. Taste and season with more salt if needed. Spoon the custardy scrambled eggs onto the toast, then sprinkle over half the chives. Crush the crisps on top and finish with the remaining chives and some freshly cracked black pepper.
Spoon the custardy scramble onto the toast, and sprinkle over half the chives. Crush the crisps on top and finish with the remaining chives and some freshly cracked black pepper.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
